core_mechanism: min-max / variational construction of an infinite tower of saddle-point critical states of a symmetry-invariant energy functional, whose persistence under a symmetry-breaking perturbation is guaranteed by comparing the growth rate of the symmetric critical-energy levels against the size of the perturbation (a Bolle-type deformation), after a saddle-point fiber reduction collapses the indefinite functional to a definite one
brief_reason: The abstract obtains quadratic-growth symmetric minimax levels, reduces the strongly indefinite problem to a C^1 path on the positive spectral space by maximizing along strictly concave negative fibers, and then invokes the polynomial Chambers--Ghoussoub--Bolle deformation theorem to yield infinitely many high-energy critical points surviving a non-even perturbation.
